name: Running tour around La Mata Lagoon from Torrevieja
name_original: Vuelta running a la Laguna de La Mata desde Torrevieja
region: Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja
region_intro: Jogging routes in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja traverse a protected wetland system spanning 3,700 hectares, characterized by its two distinct salt lagoons: the pink Laguna de Torrevieja and the green Laguna de La Mata. The landscape is semi-arid, supporting unique halophytic vegetation, with areas also featuring pine forests, eucalyptus trees, and vineyards. The park offers a network of well-signposted paths that are generally wide and lack significant slopes, making them suitable for running. Best jogging routes in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja The most popular jogging route is Running tour around La Mata Lagoon from Torrevieja, a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 55 minutes to complete. This route offers extensive views around the distinct La Mata Lagoon. Another top favourite among local runners is Laguna de La Mata – View of Laguna de La Mata loop from La Mata, a moderate 8.3 miles (13.3 km) path. This trail provides views of the Laguna de La Mata and passes through diverse plant life. Local runners also love the Running loop from Plaça del Ginjoler, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail leading through varied park scenery, often completed in about 29 minutes. Jogging in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja is defined by its unique pink and green lagoons, semi-arid landscapes, and sections of pine and eucalyptus forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging runs. The routes in Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 7000 runners have used komoot to explore Lagunas De La Mata Y Torrevieja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What makes the running routes in Lagunas de la Mata y Torrevieja unique?
A: The running routes in this natural park are defined by their distinctive landscapes, primarily the vibrant pink Laguna de Torrevieja and the green Laguna de La Mata. The semi-arid environment supports unique halophytic vegetation, alongside areas of pine forests, eucalyptus trees, and even vineyards, offering a visually diverse experience. The paths are generally wide and lack significant slopes, making them ideal for runners.

Q: Are there easy running trails for beginners in the park?
A: Yes, the park offers several easy running options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17282662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Plaça del Ginjoler&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.8 km (3.0 miles) trail that takes about 29 minutes to complete, leading through varied park scenery. Many paths are well-maintained and relatively flat, suitable for those new to running.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Lagunas de la Mata y Torrevieja?
A: Absolutely. Many of the trails in the park are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33842859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de La Mata – View of Laguna de La Mata loop from La Mata&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.3 km (8.3 miles) path offering views of the Laguna de La Mata and diverse plant life.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while jogging in the park?
A: The park is a haven for birdlife, especially around the lagoons. Joggers can often spot flamingos, storks, ducks, and black-winged stilts. There are also strategically placed &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5589759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birdwatching Huts&lt;/a&gt; for better viewing opportunities, allowing you to combine your run with nature observation.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest along the running trails?
A: Yes, the park offers several natural and man-made points of interest. You'll encounter the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/864166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mata Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; and the vibrant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3986767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pink Lake of Torrevieja&lt;/a&gt;. There's also the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4083973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Narrow trail around the Laguna de la Mata&lt;/a&gt;, which provides unique perspectives of the landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/771712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mata and Torrevieja Lagoons Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; itself is a significant attraction.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Lagunas de la Mata y Torrevieja?
A: The park is enjoyable year-round, but the vibrant pink hue of the Torrevieja lagoon intensifies in warmer months, making spring and summer particularly visually stunning. However, for comfortable running temperatures, the cooler months of autumn, winter, and early spring are often preferred, especially for longer distances.

Q: Are the running routes in the park suitable for families?
A: Yes, the park's extensive network of well-signposted paths, which are generally wide and without significant slopes, makes them very suitable for families. The varied scenery and opportunities for wildlife spotting add to the family-friendly appeal, allowing for enjoyable outings with children.

Q: Is there parking available near the jogging trails?
A: Yes, the park is easily accessible, and there is a spacious car park conveniently located near the visitor center. This makes it straightforward for those arriving by car to access the various jogging trails within the natural park.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Lagunas de la Mata y Torrevieja?
A: The running routes in Lagunas de la Mata y Torrevieja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. Runners often praise the unique landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the opportunity to observe abundant birdlife, especially flamingos, while enjoying their runs.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes available?
A: For those seeking a greater challenge, the park offers longer routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872729636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running tour around La Mata Lagoon from Torrevieja&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 17.1 km (10.6 miles) trail that takes approximately 1 hour 55 minutes to complete, offering extensive views around the La Mata Lagoon. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27637717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de La Mata loop from San Luis&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 15.9 km (9.9 miles) route.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through different types of scenery?
A: Absolutely. The park's diverse ecosystem means you can experience varied scenery on your runs. Routes often transition from open areas along the salt lakes, with their unique halophytic vegetation, to shaded sections provided by pine and eucalyptus trees, and even past vineyards, offering a dynamic visual experience.

Q: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants near the park's running areas?
A: While the park itself focuses on natural preservation, there are picnic areas for resting. The surrounding towns of Torrevieja and La Mata, which are easily accessible from the park, offer a variety of cafes, restaurants, and other amenities where you can refuel after your run.
tips: The Natural Park of the Lagoons of La Mata and Torrevieja is a protected natural area located in the province of Alicante, in the Valencian Community. This park covers more than 3,700 hectares and is made up of two main lagoons: the Laguna de La Mata and the Laguna de Torrevieja.

waypoints.name: Narrow trail around the Laguna de la Mata
